commit.message:
fix: thread review findings through implement fix loop (#69)

## Problem

GitHub downgrades `REQUEST_CHANGES` to `COMMENT` on self-PRs (bot's own
PRs). COMMENT reviews don't create resolvable threads, so
`get_unresolved_threads()` returns empty. This broke the fix loop — it
would enter based on `original_event` but immediately exit with
`partial` status because there were no threads to fix.

Additionally, on re-review rounds for self-PRs, the
`PREVIOUS_REVIEW_SECTION` was always empty (no threads to find via
GraphQL), so the reviewer had no context about what was previously
flagged.

## Solution

**Return review findings directly** — `review_pr()` now returns a
`review_threads` string (formatted from the inline comments it just
posted) in its result dict. `implement_issue()` uses these directly
instead of round-tripping through the GraphQL threads API.

**Thread context across rounds** — Previous round's findings are passed
back into the next `review_pr()` call via a `previous_comments`
parameter, so re-reviews have full context of what was flagged — even on
self-PRs where `get_unresolved_threads()` returns empty.

## Changes

- **review.py**: Add `_format_review_threads()` helper,
`previous_comments` param on `review_pr()`, fallback logic when
`get_unresolved_threads()` is empty, return `review_threads` in result
- **implement.py**: Remove `get_unresolved_threads` dependency, use
returned `review_threads` for fix prompts, thread
`previous_review_threads` across loop iterations
- **Tests**: 96 passing (3 new — format helper, no-threads partial,
previous_comments threading verification)

Co-authored-by: Colin Cheung <11246116+ColinCee@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix sprite batching to compare texnum instead of pointer

Atlas images have distinct r_image_t pointers but share the same
backing GL texture (texnum). Comparing pointers broke batching,
causing a separate glDrawElements call per atlas sub-image. Compare
texnum instead so all atlas sprites batch into a single draw call.

commit.message:
perf(interpreter): optimize object_creation_1k via StaGlobal recycling and extended fuse

Enable the zero-allocation reuse path in the SMI loop's CreateObjectLiteral
fused handler for patterns like:
  for (var i = 0; i < 1000; i++) { last = { x: i, y: i+1, z: i*2 }; }

Root cause: After Ldar+StaGlobal, the object Rc has strong_count >= 3
(register + acc + global slot), so the try_reuse_with_values check
(count == 1) never succeeds, forcing a fresh allocation every iteration.

Two complementary optimizations:

1. StaGlobal IC fast path recycling: use std::mem::replace to extract
   the old slot value before overwriting and feed it to recycle_object_rc().
   This populates the object pool for the pool-based fallback path.

2. Extended fused path: after collecting all property values, look ahead
   for a trailing Ldar rX + StaGlobal sequence. When found and the global
   IC is populated, pre-clear the spilled accumulator and global slot to
   drop extra Rc references, enabling in-place reuse (strong_count == 1).
   The fused path then writes back to the global slot and skips the
   Ldar+StaGlobal bytecodes entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace SFML input/event types with custom input abstraction

Create a backend-agnostic input system (InputTypes.h, InputSystem.h)
with an SFML implementation (SFMLInput.h/.cpp) that wraps sf::Event,
sf::Keyboard, sf::Joystick and sf::Mouse behind portable enums and
an InputEvent struct.

- Add KeyCode, MouseButton, GamepadButton, GamepadAxis enums
- Add InputSystem abstract class with static current() accessor
- SFMLRenderer creates/owns SFMLInput; Renderer exposes getInput()
- Add setWindowSize/setWindowPosition/getDesktopSize to Renderer
- Migrate InputComponent, MovementComponent to use InputSystem
- Migrate InputBindings from sf::Keyboard::Key to KeyCode
- Migrate PauseMenu, SaveSlotScreen, ControlsMenu, DebugMenu
  from sf::Event to InputEvent
- Replace sf::Clock with std::chrono-based GameClock in main.cpp
- No file outside SFMLInput.cpp and SFMLRenderer.cpp includes SFML

triggers the new Pages deploy workflow in rune-docs, publishing versioned

* ci: skip unchanged gates with path-change detection and cache Docker layers (#19)

Add a `changes` job that diffs the PR/push against its base and outputs
`python` and `docker` boolean flags. Every gate that does not need to run
on a README-only (or docs-only) commit is now guarded by an `if:` on the
relevant flag, so runners are not wasted on irrelevant work.

Docker build optimisations:
- Add `cache-from: type=gha` / `cache-to: type=gha,mode=max` to every
  `build-push-action` call so BuildKit layers are reused across jobs in
  the same workflow run and across runs when the Dockerfile/deps are
  unchanged.
- Switch `security-sbom` from plain `docker build` to `build-push-action`
  with `load: true` (required to use the GHA cache backend).
- Remove QEMU from `security-sbom` — SBOM only needs amd64.
- Decouple `smoke-cli-api` from the Python test chain so a Dockerfile-only
  change exercises the container smoke without re-running unit tests.

Merge gate already allowed `skipped` results; no change needed.

* fix(metrics): replace @contextmanager span() with class-based CM to avoid PEP-479 RuntimeError on Python 3.14

Generator-based context managers are subject to PEP-479: any StopIteration
raised inside the generator body gets converted to RuntimeError. In Python 3.14
this interacts badly with the test mock pattern
  lambda self, _: (_ for _ in ()).throw(RuntimeError(...))
which can leave stale generator/exception state that corrupts the next
@contextmanager generator's __enter__ (next(self.gen) sees the leaked
StopIteration → RuntimeError: generator raised StopIteration).

Replacing @contextmanager with a plain class (_SpanContext) eliminates the
issue entirely: __enter__/__exit__ are ordinary methods, not generators, so
PEP-479 never applies.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(metrics,tests): Literal[False] exit return type + infinite time.monotonic iterator

Two mypy/test fixes:

1. rune_bench/metrics.py: _SpanContext.__exit__ return type was 'bool'.
   mypy requires 'Literal[False]' (or None) when __exit__ always returns False,
   otherwise it infers the method may suppress exceptions — which cascades into
   'Missing return statement' on workflows.warmup_existing_ollama_model (line 89)
   because mypy thinks the 'return' inside 'with span(...)' could be unreachable.

2. tests/test_comprehensive_error_paths.py: the 2-element iterator
   iter([0.0, 10.0]) for time.monotonic was exhausted by _wait_until_instance_absent,
   but the monkeypatch targets the global time module — so the _SpanContext.__enter__
   call inside the subsequent provision_vastai_ollama span also hit this lambda and
   got StopIteration. Changed to itertools.chain(..., itertools.repeat(999.0)) so
   the iterator never exhausts while still producing the correct timeout behaviour.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix scan failures: Write-Warning leaks ANSI text to stdout in CI

Root cause: Write-Warning (PowerShell stream 3) renders ANSI-colored
text that leaks into stdout when pwsh runs in GitHub Actions. Since
stdout is redirected to scan.json.tmp, the warning text prepends the
JSON, causing 'Unexpected character at position 0' parse failures.

This affected all repos with a previous scan.json (triggering the
'cache version mismatch' warning) but not newly-added repos (which
have no previous scan file and skip the warning path).

Fix: Replace all Write-Warning calls with Write-Host in
Get-PrTriageData.ps1. Write-Host uses the information stream (stream 6)
which maps to stderr, not stdout. Add a ScriptHygiene Pester test to
prevent Write-Warning from being reintroduced.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: extend ENA poll to 20 min, add GetConsoleOutput at 5 min mark

The 10-minute timeout was too short for the two-boot sequence:
  first boot (no ENA) → detect extra-nic.npk → install → reboot
  second boot (with ENA) → DHCP → WebFig on port 80

Raised timeout to 20 min and interval to 20s.

Also added GetConsoleOutput dump at ~5 minutes for diagnostics.
ARM64 CHR kernel uses ttyAMA0 so console will likely be empty
(Graviton captures ttyS0), but the call confirms the instance is
alive and may catch any UEFI-level messages.

commit.message:
fix: remove 'from __future__ import annotations' to fix slowapi wrapper resolution

With future annotations, type hints become strings that FastAPI resolves
via the function's __globals__. slowapi's limiter.limit() decorator wraps
endpoints with a function whose __globals__ belongs to the slowapi module,
where request/response model classes don't exist. This caused 422 errors
on POST endpoints in CI (FastAPI 0.115.12 + Python 3.11).

Removing the future import makes annotations actual type objects, bypassing
the __globals__ lookup entirely.

Add Hat monotile aperiodic tiling poster generator

Implements hat_tiling_poster.py — a museum-quality poster generator for
the Hat aperiodic monotile discovered by Smith, Myers, Kaplan &
Goodman-Strauss in 2023.

Features:
- Mathematically accurate 13-vertex Hat polygon on the triangular grid
- Hierarchical cluster expansion tiling algorithm
- Two-color rendering distinguishing reflected and unreflected tiles
- Three annotation columns: Einstein Tile, Aperiodic Order, A Simple Shape
- Three educational panels: How It Tiles, The Discovery, Significance
- Full theme support (classic, blueprint, chalkboard)
- CLI with --iterations flag following the project convention

fix(browser): defer headed browser launch to avoid startup probe timeout

Azure health check window, causing 146s probe times and container
termination. Now defers the browser launch by 10s so the HTTP server
passes the startup probe first.
